verses.text: God answers Pharaoh shall be forced to let them go; by the name JEHOVAH 'was I not known' to the fathers. Made known. God reveals Himself more fully now.
God answers Pharaoh shall be forced to let them go; by the name JEHOVAH 'was I not known' to the fathers. Made known. God reveals Himself more fully now.
God answers Pharaoh shall be forced to let them go; by the name JEHOVAH 'was I not known' to the fathers. Made known. God reveals Himself more fully now.
He remembers His covenant and has heard Israel's groaning. Covenant kept. God's promise is the ground of deliverance.
He remembers His covenant and has heard Israel's groaning. Covenant kept. God's promise is the ground of deliverance.
'I will bring you out... I will redeem you... and I will take you to me for a people.' Seven I-wills. Salvation is wholly God's own work.
'I will bring you out... I will redeem you... and I will take you to me for a people.' Seven I-wills. Salvation is wholly God's own work.
'I will bring you out... I will redeem you... and I will take you to me for a people.' Seven I-wills. Salvation is wholly God's own work.
But for anguish and bondage, the people would not hear. Crushed. Suffering can dull the ear to good news.
God again sends Moses to Pharaoh, though he pleads his 'uncircumcised lips.' Renewed charge. God presses His reluctant servant onward.
God again sends Moses to Pharaoh, though he pleads his 'uncircumcised lips.' Renewed charge. God presses His reluctant servant onward.
God again sends Moses to Pharaoh, though he pleads his 'uncircumcised lips.' Renewed charge. God presses His reluctant servant onward.
God again sends Moses to Pharaoh, though he pleads his 'uncircumcised lips.' Renewed charge. God presses His reluctant servant onward.
The heads of Reuben, Simeon, and Levi are recorded. Genealogy. The deliverers' family is set in order.
The heads of Reuben, Simeon, and Levi are recorded. Genealogy. The deliverers' family is set in order.
The heads of Reuben, Simeon, and Levi are recorded. Genealogy. The deliverers' family is set in order.
The sons of Gershon, Kohath, and Merari are named, the Levite branches. Levite lines. The tribe of priesthood is traced.
The sons of Gershon, Kohath, and Merari are named, the Levite branches. Levite lines. The tribe of priesthood is traced.
The sons of Gershon, Kohath, and Merari are named, the Levite branches. Levite lines. The tribe of priesthood is traced.
Amram weds Jochebed, who bears Aaron and Moses; Izhar's and Uzziel's sons are named. The chosen house. From here come the brothers God called.
Amram weds Jochebed, who bears Aaron and Moses; Izhar's and Uzziel's sons are named. The chosen house. From here come the brothers God called.
Amram weds Jochebed, who bears Aaron and Moses; Izhar's and Uzziel's sons are named. The chosen house. From here come the brothers God called.
Aaron's sons are named, and Eleazar begets Phinehas. The priests' descent. The line of the sanctuary is recorded.
Aaron's sons are named, and Eleazar begets Phinehas. The priests' descent. The line of the sanctuary is recorded.
Aaron's sons are named, and Eleazar begets Phinehas. The priests' descent. The line of the sanctuary is recorded.
These are the Aaron and Moses whom God charged to bring Israel out. Identified. Scripture marks the men of the great deliverance.
These are the Aaron and Moses whom God charged to bring Israel out. Identified. Scripture marks the men of the great deliverance.
God speaks again; Moses repeats, 'I am of uncircumcised lips.' Still hesitant. God's purpose stands despite Moses' fear.
